title: BBE(Local) – Board Members: Authority; CH(Local) – Purchasing and Acquisition; CKC(Local) – Safety Program/Risk Management: Emergency Plans; CKE(Local) – Program/Risk Management: Security Personnel; CL(Local) – Buildings, Grounds, And Equipment Management
description: This policy area covers a range of school operations, including how board members request information, how the district purchases goods and services, emergency planning, security staffing, and management of school buildings and grounds. It affects school board members, district staff, students, and the broader community. Parents and community members should be aware of these policies because they shape how safely and efficiently schools are run — from who is responsible for security on campus to how taxpayer dollars are spent on purchases and facilities.

timeline_local.summary: Revisions to Board Policy CKE (Local) were discussed. No action was taken.
timeline_local.discussion:
Policy) — Multiple Speakers Opposed:
- **Danell Cass:** Opposed SB11; distinguished existing silent prayer rights from organized religious reading; cited low student performance in core subjects
- **Adriana Ruiz:** Called SB11 religious overreach; urged focus on education over political signaling
- **Deborah Leiber:** Person of faith opposing the policy; cited administrative burden, consent form requirements, and legal risks
- **Laurel Stevens:** Christian opposing the policy on pluralism grounds; urged preserving religious freedom
- **Destiny Milton:** Criticized lack of transparency by label
timeline_local.notes: Listed as a Board Policy Discussion Item. No action was taken. No specific change language available in these pages.

timeline_local.summary: This is a summaries page covering multiple policy changes: (1) BBE(Local): Informed by HB 4310 — requires Board member PIR requests be fulfilled promptly and at no cost; establishes new process for handling requests for potentially confidential information. (2) CH(Local): Informed by SB 12 & 1173 — permits raising aggregate purchasing threshold from $50,000 to $100,000; adds non-discrimination clause for HUBs. (3) CKC(Local): Informed by HB 121 — requires district emergency plan to include provisions for safety of students, staff, and spectators during District-sponsored/sanctioned extracurricular activities. (4) CKE(Local): Informed by HB 1458 — adds process for Board to authorize use of reserve police officers, with new section on procedures, authority, standards, and limitations. (5) CL(Local): Informed by SB 8 ('bathroom bill') — defines multiple-occupancy private spaces, requires sex-designated signage, outlines exceptions (young children, those needing assistance), requires facility use aligned with sex at birth, adds locker room as instructional classroom section, and outlines violation process.
changes_added: BBE(Local): Board member PIR requests must be fulfilled promptly and at no cost
BBE(Local): New process for handling requests for potentially confidential information
CH(Local): Aggregate purchasing threshold increase from $50,000 to $100,000 (optional for districts)
CKC(Local): Emergency plan must include provisions for safety of students, staff, and spectators during District-sponsored or District-sanctioned extracurricular activities
CKE(Local): New policy section outlining process for Board authorization of reserve police officers, including procedures, authority, standards, and limitations
CL(Local): Definition of multiple-occupancy private spaces
CL(Local): Requirement for appropriate signage consistent with sex designated for each space
CL(Local): Specific exceptions (young children, individuals requiring assistance)
CL(Local): Requirement to use facilities aligned with sex at birth
CL(Local): New section addressing use of locker rooms as instructional classrooms
CL(Local): Process for violations of the policy
The Board may authorize the chief of police of the school district police department to appoint reserve police officers for the district.

A reserve police officer shall serve at the discretion of the chief of police of the school district police department and may be called into service at any time that the chief considers it necessary to have additional officers to preserve the peace and enforce the law.
The chief of police shall only authorize a reserve police officer who meets the qualifications outlined in Education Code 37.0816 and other relevant federal, state, and local laws and regulations.

The Chief of Police shall be responsible for ensuring that reserve police officers comply with all applicable requirements, duties, and responsibilities as set forth in Education Code 37.0816 and other relevant federal, state, and local laws and regulations.
The board may limit the number of reserve police officers that the chief may appoint.
timeline_local.notes: This is a summaries/cover page (page 289, labeled page 2) from a TASB policy update packet produced by Texans for Excellence in Education. It does not contain redline text directly but summarizes changes to multiple policies. Actual redline documents for each policy would appear on subsequent pages. Action type cannot be confirmed from this page alone; marked as 'unknown' pending review of meeting agenda context. Meeting date noted as 2026-01-20.
